title.zh: 「日本国宪法在此不适用」的隐匿村落
title.en: The hidden village where 'the constitution of Japan does not apply'

summary.zh: 传说福冈县犬鸣峠的旧隧道深处有一座地图上不存在的村子，入口立牌「此后日本国宪法不适用」，闯入者有去无回。1990年代末经网络扩散。
summary.en: Beyond the old tunnel at Inunaki Pass in Fukuoka, legend places a village on no map, its entrance signed 'The constitution of Japan does not apply beyond this point.' The story spread online from the late 1990s.

description.zh:
传说的犬鸣村藏在福冈县宫若市犬鸣峠的山中：所有地图对它留白，手机进山即失去信号，通往村子的小路入口立着一块牌子——「此后，日本国宪法不适用」。村民与外界断绝数百年，见到闯入者便持镰刀锄头追杀；有说法称入口处停着一辆被砸烂的白色轿车，也有探险者声称听见深山里的犬吠与人声。这些说法在1990年代末的个人网站和BBS上定型，2000年代经手机怪谈网站与灵异节目反复放大，使犬鸣峠成为「日本第一灵异景点」候补的常客。

传说有一个可考的现实底座。犬鸣谷地确实存在过聚落，江户时代以铁山炼铁为业，1980年代因修建犬鸣水库整体迁离，1994年水库落成后旧村部分没入水底——「地图上消失的村子」由此而来。1975年新犬鸣隧道通车后，旧隧道废弃，成为暴走族与试胆者聚集地；1988年12月，五名少年在旧隧道附近将一名20岁工人殴打烧死，此案震动全国，旧隧道随后被水泥封死。真实的凶案、封死的隧道口与水下的旧村，为传说提供了全部素材。

2020年清水崇执导的电影《犬鸣村》将传说搬上银幕，日本各地随之兴起「实录系」村落怪谈的翻拍潮。现实中的犬鸣峠一带部分路段禁止进入，福冈县警多次提醒探险者止步。
description.en:
The Inunaki Village of legend hides in the mountains around Inunaki Pass in Miyawaka, Fukuoka: maps leave it blank, phone signal dies on the approach, and a sign at the mouth of the path reads, 'Beyond this point, the constitution of Japan does not apply.' Its people are said to have cut all ties with the outside for centuries and to hunt intruders with sickles and hoes; explorers' accounts add a wrecked white sedan by the entrance and dog howls and voices deep in the valley. These elements crystallized on personal websites and BBS boards in the late 1990s and were amplified through mobile horror sites and TV specials in the 2000s, making the pass a fixture of 'most haunted spot in Japan' lists.

The legend stands on a verifiable base. A real settlement existed in the Inunaki valley, including an Edo-period ironworking enclave; its residents were relocated in the 1980s for the Inunaki Dam, completed in 1994, which put part of the old village underwater, hence a village that vanished from the map. When the new Inunaki tunnel opened in 1975, the old tunnel fell derelict and drew bikers and thrill-seekers; in December 1988 five youths beat and burned to death a twenty-year-old factory worker near it, a murder that shocked the country, after which the tunnel mouth was sealed with concrete. A true crime, a walled-up tunnel, and a drowned village supplied everything the story needed.

Shimizu Takashi's 2020 film Howling Village carried the legend to cinemas and set off a wave of 'found-record' village horror. Sections of the pass are closed today, and Fukuoka police periodically warn explorers away.

value.zh: 1990年代末个人网站与BBS，2000年代手机怪谈站
value.en: Personal sites and BBS boards from the late 1990s; mobile horror sites in the 2000s

value.zh: 犬鸣水库淹没旧村（1994年）、旧隧道封闭、1988年凶杀案
value.en: Village drowned by Inunaki Dam (1994); sealed old tunnel; the 1988 murder

sources.zh: 1990年代末日本网络BBS与个人网站流传
sources.en: Late-1990s Japanese BBS and personal-website lore

sources.zh: 朝里树《日本现代怪异事典》（2018年）
sources.en: Asazato Itsuki, Nihon Gendai Kaii Jiten (2018)
